commands.repair_reason: - [P2] The blocking replay defect has a narrow code-and-test repair; maintainers can separately decide whether to retain the broader gateway shell-execution contract.; Needs attention: The patch introduces persistent gateway shell execution, and the missing durable completion state can replay that execution after restart.; Review finding: [P1] Persist completion before firing the one-shot watcher — src/gateway/cron-... (sha=6a14822437ce8c9d86b20ac813d52f3c7e91567d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an `on-exit` cron schedule that runs a gateway-owned shell command watcher and wakes the originating session when the command exits.

PR surface: Source +355, Tests +384, Docs +29. Total +768 across 27 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source: allow an enabled `on-exit` watcher to finish, restart the gateway, and startup reconciliation sees the still-enabled persisted job without any durable completion marker and launches its command again.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The blocking replay defect has a narrow code-and-test repair; maintainers can separately decide whether to retain the broader gateway shell-execution contract.
